Band External Rotation:
Stand side on to a band anchored at elbow height, holding it in the outside hand, elbow bent to 90 degrees and tucked to your side.
Start with the forearm across your body.
Keeping the elbow pinned to your side, rotate the forearm out away from your body against the band.
Squeeze at the end, then return under control.
Keep the shoulder down throughout. Swap sides.
Band Wrist Flexion:
Anchor a band low and hold it in one hand, palm facing up, forearm resting on your thigh or a bench.
Let the wrist extend back against the band.
Curl the wrist up towards you, flexing against the band.
Squeeze at the top.
Lower under control. Swap sides.
Glute Bridge Band Pull Apart:
Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat, holding a band above your chest with arms extended.
Drive through your heels to lift your hips into a glute bridge and hold.
Keeping the hips up, pull the band apart until your arms reach the floor either side.
Squeeze the shoulder blades and the glutes together.
Return the arms under control, holding the bridge throughout.
Half-Kneeling Single Arm Band Press:
Set up in a half-kneeling position side on to a band anchored at shoulder height, holding it in the hand nearest the anchor at your shoulder.
Brace your core and keep your ribs down.
Press the hand straight out in front until the arm is extended.
Resist the band trying to twist you.
Return under control. Swap sides.
Mini Band Squat:
Stand with a mini band looped around both legs just above the knees, feet about shoulder width.
Brace your core and push the knees out against the band.
Sit back and down into a squat, keeping the knees pressing out.
Go as deep as you can control.
Drive through your heels to stand back up, knees tracking over the toes.
Offset Band Barbell Reverse Lunge:
Loop a band under one foot and up over the same-side shoulder holding a barbell, or anchor the band to one side for an offset pull.
Brace your core and stand tall, resisting the pull to one side.
Step the opposite foot back into a reverse lunge, dropping the back knee towards the floor.
Drive through the front heel to stand back up.
Keep your torso level throughout. Swap sides.
Quadruped Banded Scapular Retraction:
Set up on all fours with a band looped under your hands and across your upper back.
Keep your spine neutral and arms straight.
Without bending the elbows, squeeze your shoulder blades down and together, pushing the upper back up against the band.
Hold the squeeze briefly.
Release under control and repeat.
Standing Banded Single Hip Flexion:
Anchor a band low behind you and loop it around one foot, standing tall and holding something for balance.
Brace your core.
Drive that knee up towards your chest against the band.
Squeeze at the top.
Lower under control. Swap sides.
Supine Banded Knee to Chest (Psoas Focus:
Lie on your back with a band looped around one foot, anchored low at your feet, the other leg bent or flat.
Keep your lower back flat to the floor.
Drive that knee in towards your chest against the band, driving from the hip flexor.
Hold briefly at the top.
Lower under control. Swap sides.
Cable/Band Internal Rotation Walk Outs:
Anchor a band or cable at chest height and hold it in both hands at your chest, elbows bent and rotated in so the band is taut.
Brace your core and keep the shoulders down.
Holding that internal rotation the whole time, step laterally away from the anchor to load the band, then step back.
Keep the arms locked in position throughout.
Swap sides.
Inchworm:
Stand tall, then hinge and place your hands on the floor in front of your feet.
Walk your hands forward until you reach a straight-arm plank.
Hold briefly, keeping your core braced.
Walk your feet up towards your hands in small steps.
Stand back up and repeat.
Mini Band Bodyweight Bench Press:
Lie back on a bench with a mini band looped around both wrists, arms pressed up over your chest.
Set your hands about shoulder width so the band is under tension.
Brace your core and keep your ribs down.
Press your hands straight up and slightly together, driving against the band.
Lower under control back over your chest, keeping the band taut.
Monkey Bar of Death:
Set a barbell in a rack between knee and hip height.
Stand and place the back of one thigh on top of the bar, hamstring resting on it.
Ease your weight down onto the bar.
Slowly shift along the hamstring to find a tender spot, or bend and straighten the knee to floss the tissue.
Pause and breathe on tight spots. Swap sides.
Supine From Hands Banded Hip Abduction:
Sit on the floor leaning back on your hands, knees bent and feet flat, a band looped around your thighs just above the knees.
Brace your core and keep your chest up.
Drive your knees apart against the band.
Squeeze at the end, then return under control.
Keep the feet planted throughout.
Supine Foam Hands Banded Hip Abduction:
Sit on the floor leaning back on your hands, knees bent and feet flat, a band looped around your thighs just above the knees.
Brace your core and keep your chest up.
Drive your knees apart against the band.
Squeeze at the end, then return under control.
Keep the feet planted throughout.
